LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: COMPLIANCE WITH INVESTIGATORY SUBPOENA IS CONTINUED FROM DEC-14-2016 TO JAN-13-2017, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302, PENDING JUDGE GOLDBERG'S RULING ON THE MOTION TO ENFORCE PROTECTIVE ORDER. EACH PARTY MAY FILE A STATUS BRIEF BY JANUARY 11, 2017 WITH A COURTESY COPY EITHER HAND-DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302 THAT DAY OR SENT TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. COUNSEL FOR THE INTERVENORS IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER CONTINUING THE MATTER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER THE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S PETITION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH THE INVESTIGATORY SUBPOENA SERVED ON RESPONDENT TEVA, INC. IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 21, 2017, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPARTMENT 302 FOR ENTRY OF ORDER. ALL OBJECTIONS BY THE "END PLAYERS" ARE OVERRULED WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THOSE PERTAINING TO PRIVATE FINANCIAL INFORMATION OF THE NAMED PLAINTIFFS, END PLAYERS ON THE EAST COAST. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO MEET, CONFER AND PREPARE A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER ACCORDING TO THIS DATE'S PROCEEDINGS, OR ALTERNATIVELY THEY ARE TO SUBMIT RESPECTIVE OBJECTIONS TO [PROPOSED] COMPETING ORDERS. NO APPEARANCE SHALL BE NECESSARY ON FEBRUARY 21, 2017 IF AN ORDER IS SIGNED AND ENTERED PRIOR TO THAT DATE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena
Off Calendar Mar-03-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Terrance Ziegler, of Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP, Tel. 610.822.2210, for intervenor, Vista Healthplan Inc., appearing via CourtCall;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena is taken off calendar pursuant to the proposed Stipulation and Order resolving the matter. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena
Off Calendar Feb-21-2017 Continued To Mar-03-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena is CONTINUED to March 3, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 to give the parties additional time to work out the remaining issues, per the February 16, 2017 letter received from counsel for petitioner.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Continued To Feb-21-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy Attorney General, Adam Miller of the California Department of Justice (415.703.5551) appears as counsel for petitioner

Attorney, Michael P. Esser of the law offices of Kirkland & Ellis LLP (415.439.1972) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party

Attorney, Terence Ziegler of the law offices of Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check LLP (610.822.2210) appears via telephone as counsel for intervenor, Vista Healthplan, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Petitioner The Attorney General's petition to compel compliance with the investigatory subpoena served on respondent Teva, Inc. is CONTINUED to February 21, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 for entry of order. All objections by the "end players" are overruled with the exception of those pertaining to private financial information of the named plaintiffs, end players on the east coast. The parties are directed to meet, confer and prepare a [Proposed] written order according to this date's proceedings, or alternatively they are to submit respective objections to [proposed] competing orders. No appearance shall be necessary on February 21, 2017 if an order is signed and entered prio to that date.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena
Off Calendar Jan-13-2017 Continued To Jan-26-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena is continued on the court's own motion to January 26, 2017. Judge Goldberg's office has stated that he will be issuing a ruling shortly. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Intervene
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adam Miller of Office of the Attorney General telephone number 415-703-5551 for Petitioner CA Attorney General.
Paul Breucop of Kessler Topaz Meltzer Check LLP telephone number 415-400-3012 for Intervenors End Payor.
Michael Esser of Kirkland & Ellis LLP telephone number 415-439-1973 for Respondent Teva, Inc.

The matter is uncontested. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Proposed intervenors Vista Healthplan, Inc., Pennsylvania Turnpike Commission, Shirley Panebianco, Pennsylvania Employees Benefit Trust Fund and District Council 37 Health & Security Plan's motion for leave to intervene is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Continued To Jan-13-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adam Miller of Office of the Attorney General telephone number 415-703-5551 for Petitioner CA Attorney General.
Paul Breucop of Kessler Topaz Meltzer Check LLP telephone number 415-400-3012 for Intervenors End Payor.
Michael Esser of Kirkland & Ellis LLP telephone number 415-439-1973 for Respondent Teva, Inc.

The matter was argued. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. The Order To Show Cause Re: Compliance With Investigatory Subpoena is continued to January 13, 2017 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 pending Judge Goldberg's ruling on the motion to enforce protective order. Each party may file a status brief by January 11, 2017 with a courtesy copy either hand-delivered to Department 302 that day or sent to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Counsel for the Intervenors is to prepare an order continuing the matter and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
